title: fix: include usage in OpenAI image responses
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds OpenAI-compatible token usage to image generation and edit responses.
- Preserves internal image tracking headers when binary responses are wrapped as JSON.
- Covers response mapping and billing behavior with focused tests.

Tests:
- Focused image usage unit test
- Focused OpenAI image generation integration test
- Gen TypeScript typecheck

gist.headline: Images now count themselves
gist.blurb: OpenAI-compatible image responses now bring their usage receipts along. The pixels arrived; now the accounting does too.
gist.summary: OpenAI-compatible image generation and edit responses now include token usage data while preserving internal image-tracking headers when binary image results are returned as JSON.
gist.impact: Developers can track usage and billing from OpenAI-style image responses without losing the headers used to associate generated images with their requests.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy API accounting scene: Polly is carefully attaching a tiny usage receipt to a framed generated pixel landscape, while a friendly CRT monitor robot verifies the image's tracking tags. A small code-plant and blooming flowers make the technical bookkeeping feel like garden maintenance rather than tax season.
gist.image_prompt: A cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden hackerspace: the main character is a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings; Polly wears tiny accountant glasses and carefully clips a pixel-font usage receipt onto a framed generated landscape while a cute chunky CRT-monitor robot checks glowing tracking labels. Lime green (#ecf874) is prominent on the terminal, receipt glow, and code-plants growing among pastel flowers; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle scanlines, with simple pixel text: 'USAGE OK'.
